message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Children’s attendances in paediatric emergency departments have fallen precipitously in North East England and elsewhere in recent months. We analysed data from 3 hospitals to understand which children were not being brought during the COVID-19 ‘lockdown’. In our population there is no evidence of a disproportionate impact on children belonging to vulnerable sociodemographic groups and no obvious change in illness acuity among those children still attending. However we noted a marked reduction in infectious disease presentations which might reflect one positive impact of enhanced social distancing on child health. More granular data describing the ‘collateral damage’ of the COVID-19 pandemic to children’s clinical services are needed to plan for the mitigation of its continuing effects.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What is known on this topic</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Presentations to paediatric emergency departments in Europe and the United States have reduced dramatically during the COVID-19 pandemic ‘lockdown’.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What this paper adds box</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>This is the first paper to show that reduced attendance was proportionate across different deprivation and ethnicity groups.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>We show that presentations of children with infectious diseases reduced more than those with other conditions or trauma.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>There was no change in admission rates, taken as a broad indicator of illness acuity at presentation among the population still attending paediatric emergency departments.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Equity in Paediatric Emergency Departments during COVID-19

reference.unstructured: COVID-19 - resources for parents and carers. RCPCH. https://www.rcpch.ac.uk/resources/covid-19-resources-parents-carers (accessed 21 May 2020).
